quality.rationale: 基于弱场慢速近似的多步泰勒展开与流体力学守恒律推导，逻辑链条完整且具天体物理应用背景

origin.question: Problem 14.8. Show that the relativistic Bernoulli equation of Problem 14.7 reduces to the correct Newtonian limit for slow velocities and weak gravitational fields.
origin.answer: \frac {1}{2} v ^ {2} + \phi + \pi + p / \rho_ {0} = c o n s t a n t.
origin.solution: Solution 14.8. The Newtonian limit of relativistic hydrodynamics is obtained by choosing a global, nearly-Lorentz, frame in which$$\begin{array}{l} \rho = \rho_{0} (1 + \pi), \quad \pi \ll 1 \\ \mathbf {g}_{0 0} = - (1 + 2 \phi), \quad | \phi | \ll 1 \\ p / \rho_{0} \stackrel {\cdot} {<} < 1, \quad v^{2} \ll 1. \\ \end{array}$$Here $\rho_0 = \mathfrak{nm}_{\mathbf{B}}$ is the rest-mass density ( $\mathfrak{m}_{\mathbf{B}} = \text{mean baryon rest mass}$ ), $\pi$ is the specific internal energy, $\phi$ is the Newtonian potential and $\mathbf{v}$ is 3-velocity of the fluid. Since$$\mathbf {u}^{\widehat {0}} = \left(1 - \mathbf {v}^{2}\right)^{- \frac {1}{2}} \approx 1 + \frac {1}{2} \mathbf {v}^{2},$$and$$\mathsf {u}_{0} = \left(- \mathsf {g}_{0 0}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \mathsf {u}^{\hat {0}} \approx 1 + \frac {1}{2} \mathsf {v}^{2} + \phi ,$$the Bernoulli equation becomes$$\left(1 + \frac {1}{2} v^{2} + \phi\right) = \frac {\text {c o n s t a n t} \times n}{\rho_{0} (1 + \pi + p / \rho_{0})}$$that is$$\left(1 + \frac {1}{2} v^{2} + \phi\right) \left(1 + \pi + p / \rho_{0}\right) = c o n s t a n t$$and finally$$\frac {1}{2} v^{2} + \phi + \pi + p / \rho_{0} = c o n s t a n t.$$
